evidence: Paper coins the term 'Posttranslational Modification Inspired Drug Design (PTMI-DD)' and lays out its four operational strategies (covalent / induced-pocket / PPI / degrader).

Paper presents asciminib (ABL001) myristoyl-pocket allosteric Bcr-Abl inhibition, tazemetostat EZH2 inhibition, HDAC inhibitor approvals, thalidomide/CRBN degraders, and a self-reported covalent RhoA inhibitor as evidence that targeting PTM-modified isoforms yields better selectivity and expands the druggable space.
